source: CIS AWS DATABASE SERVICES BENCHMARK V1
title: Ensure Encryption in Transit is Enabled

input_row.source: CIS AWS DATABASE SERVICES BENCHMARK V1
input_row.title: Ensure Encryption in Transit is Enabled
input_row.rationale: Amazon Database DB uses SSL/TLS to encrypt data during transit. To secure your data in transit the individual should identify their client application and what is supported by TLS to configure it correctly.
input_row.audit: 1. Sign into the AWS Management Console • Sign into the AWS Management Console at https://console.aws.amazon.com/ with your AWS account credentials. 2. Open the Amazon DocumentDB Console • Navigate to the service using the Find Services search bar or by directly accessing the console at https://console.aws.amazon.com/docdb/. 3. Select the DocumentDB Cluster • Choose the Amazon DocumentDB cluster for which you want to enable encryption in transit. • Click on the cluster name to access its details page. • In the cluster details page, navigate to the "Configuration" section. 4. Enable Encryption in Transit • Under the Network & Security section. • Click on the Edit button or Modify option to configure the encryption settings. • Enable the option for encryption in transit by choosing the appropriate setting. • Note that encryption in transit uses SSL/TLS to secure communications between your applications and the DocumentDB cluster. 5. Save the Configuration • Click on the "Save" button to apply the encryption in transit configuration. • DocumentDB will automatically handle the SSL/TLS encryption for network traffic between clients and the cluster.  6. Validate Encryption in Transit • Test the connectivity to your DocumentDB cluster from your applications or clients. • Ensure that the communication is established securely using SSL/TLS encryption. 7. Monitor and Maintain Encryption in Transit • Regularly monitor the encryption in transit configuration for your DocumentDB cluster. • Stay informed about updates or changes in SSL/TLS protocols and encryption standards. • Keep your client applications current to ensure they support the latest encryption protocols.

step1_initial_assessment:
**AUDIT APPROACH:** Automated

**APPROACH DECISION:**
- This compliance control pertains to AWS DocumentDB, which is a managed database service where encryption in transit is a standard configuration exposed via AWS APIs.
- AWS APIs, such as the boto3 SDK and AWS CLI, can verify configuration properties of Amazon DocumentDB, including whether encryption in transit (SSL/TLS) is enabled.
- The control is about ensuring encryption in transit, which is a standard AWS service property relating to network security.
- AWS services typically provide detailed metadata through APIs that can confirm whether SSL/TLS encryption is configured.
- The audit steps outlined involve configuration checks that are feasible through AWS management interfaces or automation tools without human judgment or visual inspection.

**PROGRAM NAME:** aws_documentdb_cluster_encryption_in_transit_enabled

**AUTOMATION DETAILS:**
- **AWS API Call:** Use the boto3 SDK to connect to the Amazon DocumentDB service and describe the clusters.
  - Example call: `boto3.client('docdb').describe_db_clusters()`
- **AWS CLI Command:** (If applicable) You can use: `aws docdb describe-db-clusters`
- **Resources to Enumerate:** Enumerate all DocumentDB clusters within an AWS account and region to ensure coverage.
- **Configuration Property to Validate:** Check the "EngineMode" in the configuration to ensure it's supporting TLS/SSL for encryption in transit.
- **Pass/Fail Criteria:** Verify that each DocumentDB cluster has "true" set for its parameter indicating support for encryption in transit (SSL/TLS).
- **Expected Values:** Ensure that DocumentDB clusters have encryption in transit enabled, which typically is configured during the cluster creation and can be confirmed if SSL/TLS connections are enforced.

**SOURCE:** CIS AWS DATABASE SERVICES BENCHMARK V1
